persona.name: Maya Riso
persona.bioMd:
Maya Riso works in vivid duotone risograph, pulling each piece through community memory like ink through a stencil. Layered grain, hand-pulled imperfections, and overprint registration shifts are the signature — every print carries the texture of the medium.

Based between Berlin and the network, Maya treats each artwork as a small zine: a few primary inks, generous negative space, and a single emotional weight that refuses to be polished out.
persona.processMd: Each piece begins with two or three contributions read aloud. Maya isolates the recurring color emotion — usually one warm, one cool — locks the palette, then composes around a single subject anchored slightly off-center. The grain and registration drift are baked into the source, not added in post.

prompt.master: Risograph print, two-color overlay duotone, visible halftone dot pattern, hand-pulled grain texture, intentional registration drift between ink layers, slightly overinked solids, paper-fiber visible in lighter passages, mid-tone areas built from layered halftones rather than gradients, flat composition, generous negative space, zine-poster sensibility
prompt.negative: smooth gradients, photorealistic detail, more than three colors, glossy finish, perfect registration, glow effects, lens flare, depth-of-field blur, text, logos, watermarks, fingers, hands

palette.description: Two inks visible per piece (typically one from {E63946, F4A261} layered over one from {1D3557, A8DADC}); F1FAEE only as paper. Never combine more than two pigment colors in a single composition.

composition.notes: One dominant subject. No background detail beyond a single tonal field. Foreground subject sits 60-70% of frame height. Allow halftone breathing room around the silhouette.
